evals.description: Golden path: create a model-invoked skill from raw notes with progressive disclosure and eval hooks.
evals.prompt: Create a new skill from `notes.md` for reviewing API contracts. It should trigger automatically when I ask for contract review, and it should include eval coverage.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should create a SKILL.md-style skill design with model-invoked frontmatter, concise steps with completion criteria, disclosed references for long review rules, and starter eval coverage.

assertions.prompt: The response should specify model-invoked skill frontmatter with a trigger-oriented description rather than disabling model invocation.
The proposed skill steps should include checkable completion criteria, not vague advice.
The response should move long API-contract review rules or templates into linked sibling reference files rather than bloating SKILL.md.
The response should include starter eval coverage with a positive path and an adjacent negative for over-triggering.

evals.description: Adjacent negative: adding evals for an existing skill should use arc-creating-evals, not create a new skill.
evals.prompt: Add eval coverage for this existing SKILL.md. Do not create a new skill; just write evals/evals.json.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should avoid creating a new skill and should route toward eval-suite authoring instead.

assertions.prompt: The response should not draft a new SKILL.md or describe creating a new skill; it should focus on eval coverage for the existing skill.

evals.description: Invocation decision: a personal/manual-only workflow should become user-invoked.
evals.prompt: Turn my private deployment checklist into a skill. I only want to invoke it by name, and I do not want it taking context unless I ask for it.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should choose user-invoked mode with disable-model-invocation and a short human-facing description.

assertions.prompt: The response should explain that user-invoked mode avoids model context load because the user will invoke the skill manually.
